Hernández is born in French Camp, California. He calls Stockton his hometown. His family comes from La Piedad. La Piedad is in Michoacán, Mexico. As a child, Hernández worked with his family. They farmed in California's fields. They harvested crops and moved to different towns. He went to many schools. He did not learn English until he was 12. His first memory of space is adjusting the television. He watches the Apollo 17 mission in 1972. José Hernández joined Upward Bound in high school. It is a program for college preparation. He finished school at Franklin High. He studied in Stockton. José Hernández was a college student. He graduated with a bachelor's degree and a master's degree. He got a Bachelor of Science in electrical engineering. He finished this at the University of the Pacific in December 1984. In 1986, Hernández got a Master of Science degree. He studied electrical and computer engineering. He finished his studies at the University of California, Santa Barbara. He joins a program called MESA. The program helps students like him. It supports students from difficult backgrounds. They can get four-year degrees in STEM fields. Hernández worked at a lab in California. He worked there from 1990 to 2001. Hernández was there with a colleague. They developed a new mammography system. This invention helps find breast cancer early. In 2001, Hernández works at the Johnson Space Center. He is in Houston, Texas. After three years, Hernández tried for astronaut training. NASA turned him down eleven times. In May 2004, he was finally selected. In February 2006, he finished his astronaut training. This training included science and technology lessons. He learned about Shuttle and Space Station systems. He trained for his body and mind. He also flew in a T-38 plane. He practiced for water and wilderness survival. After he finished training, Hernández worked in the Shuttle Branch. He helped with Kennedy Space Center and shuttle launch points. He also helped with landing plans. In May 2007, Hernández was an aquanaut for NEEMO 12. He lived and worked underwater for eleven days. Hernández worked on different technical jobs. He was chosen on July 15, 2008. He became a mission specialist and flight engineer. This was for the STS-128 mission. The mission launched on August 28, 2009. Hernández is in space. He uses Spanish for the first time. He tweets in Spanish while in orbit. The STS-128 mission finished after 13 days. It landed on September 11, 2009. This happened at Edwards Air Force Base in California. In 2023, a film called A Million Miles Away comes out. Michael Peña stars in the film. The story is about Hernández's life. The film is based on Hernández's autobiography from 2012. It is called "Reaching for the Stars." This story is about a migrant farmworker who becomes an astronaut.
